title: sleepy.py: 'no-op' false positive when Morty produces uncommitted work
source_context: OpenRick project, OPRK-010 overnight, 7m41s wall time, ~20k tokens spent per sleepy's burn estimate
description: During an overnight sleepy run on OPRK-010, architect-morty produced a valid event-bus refactor (new core/events.py, refactored agent.py + cli.py, ADR-001, +16 tests, all gates green) but never invoked git commit. Sleepy's outcome check ('post_sha == pre_sha → no-op, delete branch') deleted the sleepy/OPRK-010 branch. The unstaged changes carried over to main on the subsequent 'git checkout main'. Net result: 7h41m of work labelled no-op, no branch in QUEUE.md, no token attribution, no review path. The pre-commit hook stash-conflict pattern (also seen during foreground dispatches) is the suspected root cause of the missed commit.
proposed_changes: Three fixes: (1) Before declaring no-op, also check 'git diff --quiet' and 'git diff --cached --quiet'. If either shows uncommitted work, stage + commit it on the sleepy branch with a synthetic message like 'OPRK-NNN: [sleepy auto-commit — Morty did not commit]' before queueing. (2) Stamp the delegate prompt with a 'COMMIT YOUR WORK' reminder + a final 'git status' echo so the Morty knows to commit before exiting. (3) Make sleepy run delegate from inside the sleepy branch with HEAD detached or use stash+pop to guarantee changes don't escape branch context.
reject_reason: Apply failed: Evolve timed out after 300s
